heading: Report of arrival of vessels, vehicles, and aircraft
label: 1433 Report of arrival of vessels, vehicles, and aircraft
notes: Prior ProvisionsProvisions similar to those in this section were contained in , . That section was superseded by section 433 of act , comprising this section, and repealed by section 651(a)(1) of the 1930 act.act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 43342 Stat. 951June 17, 1930R.S. § 2774, requiring a report of arrival, and a further report in the form of a manifest, and imposing a penalty for violations was superseded by , , and repealed by section 642 of that act.act Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356, title IV, § 43342 Stat. 951R.S. § 2772, relative to report and entry by the master of every vessel, bound to a port of delivery; section 2775, requiring a special report by the master of any vessel having on board distilled spirits or wines; and section 2832, relative to report of arrival of vessels proceeding to the ports of Natchez or Vicksburg, were also repealed by .section 642 of the act of Sept. 21, 1922, ch. 356

Amendments2000—Subsec. (a)(1)(C). struck out “bonded merchandise, or” before “foreign merchandise”.Pub. L. 106–4761993—Subsec. (a)(1)(D). , added subpar. (D).Pub. L. 103–182, § 652(1)Subsec. (d). , substituted “present, or transmit pursuant to an electronic data interchange system, to the Customs Service such information, data,” for “present to customs officers such”.Pub. L. 103–182, § 652(2)Subsec. (e). , amended subsec. (e) generally. Prior to amendment, subsec. (e) read as follows: “Unless otherwise authorized by law, a vessel, aircraft, or vehicle may, after arriving in the United States or the Virgin Islands—Pub. L. 103–182, § 652(3)“(1) depart from the port, place, or airport of arrival; or“(2) discharge any passenger or merchandise (including baggage);only in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary.”1986— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section read as follows: “Within twenty-four hours after the arrival of any vessel from a foreign port or place, or of a foreign vessel from a domestic port, or of a vessel of the United States carrying bonded merchandise, or foreign merchandise for which entry has not been made, at any port or place within the United States at which such vessel shall come to, the master shall, unless otherwise provided by law, report the arrival of the vessel at the nearest customhouse, under such regulations as the Commissioner of Customs may prescribe.”Pub. L. 99–570

children.label: A
children.text: any vessel from a foreign port or place;

children.label: B
children.text: any foreign vessel from a domestic port;

children.label: C
children.text: any vessel of the United States carrying foreign merchandise for which entry has not been made; or

children.label: D
children.text: any vessel which has visited a hovering vessel or received merchandise while outside the territorial sea;

children.label: A
children.text: prescribe the manner in which arrivals are to be reported under paragraph (1); and

children.label: B
children.text: extend the time in which reports of arrival must be made, but not later than 24 hours after arrival.
Vehicles may arrive in the United States only at border crossing points designated by the Secretary.

children.label: B
children.text: present the vehicle, and all persons and merchandise (including baggage) on board, for inspection;

children.heading: Aircraft arrival
children.label: c Aircraft arrival
children.text: The pilot of any aircraft arriving in the United States or the Virgin Islands from any foreign airport or place shall comply with such advance notification, arrival reporting, and landing requirements as the Secretary may by regulation prescribe.

children.heading: Presentation of documentation
children.label: d Presentation of documentation
children.text: The master, person in charge of a vehicle, or aircraft pilot shall present, or transmit pursuant to an electronic data interchange system, to the Customs Service such information, data, documents, papers, or manifests as the Secretary may by regulation prescribe.

children.heading: Prohibition on departures and discharge
children.label: e Prohibition on departures and discharge

children.text: depart from the port, place, or airport of arrival; or
discharge any passenger or merchandise (including baggage).
